title: Mizuho Raises Affirm Price Target to $100 on GMV Outlook
text:
Mizuho Raises Affirm Price Target to $100

Published: May 13, 2026

Key Details

Mizuho Securities elevated its price target for Affirm Holdings (NASDAQ: AFRM) to $100 from the previous $95, while maintaining an Outperform rating. The stock currently trades at $65.43 with a $21.9 billion market capitalization.

Rationale

The firm's decision centered on Affirm's ambitious growth framework. According to the analysis, "the company's $100 billion gross merchandise volume target, which implies a 25% compound annual growth rate" represents a significant expansion opportunity.

Mizuho identified additional revenue drivers through Affirm Edge and Agentic partnerships, which could generate revenue less transaction costs reaching 7-8%, compared to the core business's 3.75-4.0% margin.

The firm noted an important economic sensitivity: "every 100 basis points of lower interest rates should translate into 40 basis points of higher GMV." This relationship could meaningfully impact profitability.

Broader Context

Multiple analysts have recently upgraded Affirm, including Needham (raised to $90) and Evercore ISI (maintained $90 target). The company also announced integration of buy-now-pay-later options into Google Search and Gemini, expanding payment accessibility.
